request.question: The actor who starred in "Three Socks" and "Hustle" also starred in a miniseries with Anglea Bassett that is set in what city?

title: Josh Peck
Jeremy Sisto
Robert Glenister
Close to the Enemy
Matt Di Angelo
David Huynh
Laura Marano
Bassett Creek (Crystal, Minnesota)
Low Winter Sun (U.S. TV series)
Riz Ahmed
sentences: Joshua Michael Peck (born November 10, 1986) is an American actor, voice actor, comedian and YouTube personality.
He is known for playing Josh Nichols in the Nickelodeon live-action sitcom "Drake & Josh".
He began his career as a child actor in the late 90s and early 2000s, and became known to young audiences after his role on "The Amanda Show".
He has since acted in films such as "Mean Creek", "Drillbit Taylor", "The Wackness", "ATM", and "Red Dawn", along with voicing Eddie in the "Ice Age" franchise.
He also starred as Gerald in a lead role with John Stamos in the series "Grandfathered".
He currently voices Casey Jones in "Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles".
He also starred in a Netflix comedy, "Take the 10", along with Tony Revolori.
Jeremy Merton Sisto (born October 6, 1974) is an American actor, producer, and writer.
Sisto has had recurring roles as Billy Chenowith on the HBO series "Six Feet Under" (2001–05), and as NYPD Detective Cyrus Lupo on NBC's drama series "Law & Order" (2008–10).
He also starred in the comedy "Clueless" (1995), the biblical miniseries "Jesus" (1999), the drama "Thirteen" (2003), and the horror film "Wrong Turn" (2003).
In 2004, he starred as bigoted baseball player Shane Mungitt in "Take Me Out", for which he was nominated for a Los Angeles Drama Critics Circle Award for Featured Performance in a Play.
In 2006, he starred in the play "Festen" on Broadway.

Robert Lewis Glenister (born 11th March 1960) is an English actor known for his roles as con man Ash "Three Socks" Morgan in the BBC television series "Hustle" and Nicholas Blake in the BBC spy drama "Spooks".
Close to the Enemy is a British period drama miniseries set in the late 1940s in London.

It is written and directed by Stephen Poliakoff, starring Jim Sturgess, Freddie Highmore, Charlotte Riley, Phoebe Fox, Alfred Molina, Lindsay Duncan, August Diehl, Alfie Allen, Angela Bassett, Antje Traue, Lily G and Robert Glenister.
It premiered in the United Kingdom on BBC Two on 10 November 2016.
Mario Angelo Constantinou (born 1 May 1987), professionally known as Matt Di Angelo, is a British actor and singer, best known for his role as Dean Wicks in the BBC soap opera "EastEnders".
He also starred as Sean Kennedy in the BBC drama series "Hustle".
David Huynh (born February 5, 1983) is a Canadian actor.

Huynh won a Special Jury Prize Award at the 2007 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival for Outstanding Newcomer and Best Emerging Actor for his performance in Juwan Chung's "Baby", which also won a Special Jury Award for Best Feature Length Film at that year's Festival.
Huynh has also Starred in Byron Q's film, "Bang Bang" (2011), which won a Special Jury Award for Best First Feature, Narrative, at the 2011 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival.
He also played Jonathan Chen in Byron Q's film, "Las Vegas Story" and Ty Do in Mark Tran's film "All About Dad".
He recently stars in a Web Series entitled "Hollywood Aliens" from Beyond Cinema Productions, directed by "Bang Bang" Director Byron Q and Kevin Boston.

He also appears as the protagonist "Fong" in a pilot entitled "Chinatown Squad" about 1890s San Francisco Chinatown, directed and produced by Stephane Gauger and written by and starring "Baby" co-star Feodor Chin, who plays the antagonist, "Pistol Pete."

He has also starred as Benson Fong in Timothy Tau's short film bio-pic Keye Luke, which premiered at the 2012 Los Angeles Asian Pacific Film Festival and which was Closing Night Film of the inaugural 2013 Seattle Asian American Film Festival.
Laura Marie Marano (born November 29, 1995) is an American actress and singer.
She starred in the Disney Channel series "Austin & Ally" as Ally Dawson.
Marano was one of the five original classmates in "Are You Smarter than a 5th Grader?
She starred in "Without a Trace" for three seasons and also "Back to You", in both instances playing the daughter of the main characters.
Marano starred in the indie film "A Sort of Homecoming".
Marano also starred in the 2015 Disney Channel Original Movie "Bad Hair Day" along with "Good Luck Charlie" actress Leigh-Allyn Baker.
In 2015, she signed with Big Machine Records and released her debut single "Boombox" on March 11, 2016.
At the end of 2016, Big Machine Records made the decision to drop all of their pop artists.
Marano then signed to Warner Bros.
Records in 2017 and plans to release her debut album with the label.
She is also the younger sister of actress Vanessa Marano who starred in Freeform's "Switched at Birth".
Bassett Creek is a neighborhood within the city of Crystal, Minnesota.

It borders the city of Minneapolis to the south and east, and is generally considered to be the section of Crystal that is situated east of Douglas Drive, south of 36th Ave. N., and west of Highway 100.
Bassett Creek is bordered by Crystal neighborhoods Lee Park to the east, Valley Place to the west, and Fair to the north.
The neighborhood is the location for Bassett Creek Park, the largest park in Crystal.
The park has a 3,700 sq. ft. playground, a baseball field, softball field, hockey rink, four volleyball courts, three tennis courts, 12 holes of disc golf and 1.06 miles of asphalt trails.
A neighborhood association has been formed to assist in the upkeep of the park facilities.
The neighborhood is also part of the Bassett Creek Watershed, and is the location of the Bassett Park Pond sub-watershed.
The watershed is overseen by the Bassett Creek Watershed Management Commission.
Low Winter Sun is an American crime drama television series that aired on AMC from August 11 to October 6, 2013, for one season consisting of ten episodes.
The series was developed by Chris Mundy and starred Mark Strong and Lennie James.
It is based upon the 2006 British two-part miniseries of the same name which also starred Strong in the same role.
Filmed and set in Detroit, Michigan, the series follows detectives Frank Agnew and Joe Geddes after they murder a corrupt cop and attempt to cover it up, and explores organized crime in Detroit.
The series received generally mixed reviews and AMC announced in December 2013 that it had canceled the series.
Rizwan Ahmed (Urdu: ‎ ; born 1 December 1982), also known as Riz MC, is a British-Pakistani actor, rapper and activist.
As an actor, he won an Emmy Award, out of two Emmy nominations, and was also nominated for a Golden Globe, Screen Actors Guild Award, and three British Independent Film Awards.

He was initially known for his work in independent films such as "The Road to Guantanamo" (2006), "Shifty" (2008), "Four Lions" (2010), "Trishna" (2011), and "Ill Manors" (2012), before his breakout role in "Nightcrawler" (2014).
In 2016, he starred in "Una", "Jason Bourne", and as Bodhi Rook in the first "Star Wars" "Anthology" film, "Rogue One".
That year, he also starred in the HBO miniseries "The Night Of" as Nasir Khan; the show and his performance were critically lauded.

At the 2017 Emmy Awards, he received two nominations, for his performance in "The Night Of" and his guest spot in "Girls"; he won the award for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Limited Series or Movie for "The Night Of", becoming the first Asian and first Muslim to win in the category, the first South Asian male to win an acting Emmy, and the first Muslim and first South Asian to win a lead acting Emmy.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: The actor who starred in "Three Socks" and "Hustle" also starred in a miniseries with Anglea Bassett that is set in what city?
hop1_docs: The Chef, the Actor, the Scoundrel | The Chef, the Actor, the Scoundrel is a 2013 Chinese comedy film directed by Guan Hu, starring Liu Ye, Zhang Hanyu and Huang Bo.  The film is meant to be "mass entertainment", in the words of Guan, who also co-wrote the screenplay.  The film is set in 1942, during the Second Sino-Japanese War.  Much of the plot revolves around the fates of two Japanese scientists who are captured in Beiping (Beijing) by the three title characters, who have trouble deciding what to do with them.

Robert Glenister | Robert Lewis Glenister (born 11th March 1960) is an English actor known for his roles as con man Ash "Three Socks" Morgan in the BBC television series "Hustle" and Nicholas Blake in the BBC spy drama "Spooks".

Alessandro Nivola | Alessandro Antine Nivola (born June 28, 1972) is an American actor and producer, who has starred in feature films such as "Face/Off", "American Hustle", "A Most Violent Year", and also a brief appearance in "Selma".

My Body, My Child | My Body, My Child is a 1982 television film directed by Marvin J. Chomsky and starring Vanessa Redgrave.  It premiered on ABC on 12 April 1982.  It includes early performances by future "Sex and the City" co-stars, Sarah Jessica Parker and Cynthia Nixon.  It is also the final television role of Jack Albertson, who was subsequently nominated posthumously for the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Miniseries or a Movie.

Close to the Enemy | Close to the Enemy is a British period drama miniseries set in the late 1940s in London.  It is written and directed by Stephen Poliakoff, starring Jim Sturgess, Freddie Highmore, Charlotte Riley, Phoebe Fox, Alfred Molina, Lindsay Duncan, August Diehl, Alfie Allen, Angela Bassett, Antje Traue, Lily G and Robert Glenister.  It premiered in the United Kingdom on BBC Two on 10 November 2016.

Science of Star Wars (miniseries) | Science of Star Wars is a 2005 TV documentary miniseries aired by the Discovery Channel.  The series features mainly archive footage from the actors of the original and prequel trilogies of the "Star Wars" saga.

The Legendary Ranger | The Legendary Ranger (, Yuan Zhen-xia) is a 1993 Hong Kong cross-genre television miniseries produced by Mui Siu-ching and aired by TVB.  The series starred Leon Lai, Michelle Reis and Catherine Hung, and featured a supporting cast of veteran actors, stars, and future stars, including Faye Wong and Athena Chu.  It is based on Ni Kuang's fictional surgeon/adventurer Yuan Zhen-xia and is set in the Wisely fictional universe, a setting in which extraterrestrial aliens, magic and wuxia heroes co-exist.
hop1_titles: The Chef, the Actor, the Scoundrel
response.summary_1: Passage [2] identifies Robert Glenister as the actor who played con man "Ash 'Three Socks' Morgan" in the BBC television series "Hustle" (quoted: "Ash 'Three Socks' Morgan in the BBC television series 'Hustle'"). Passage [5] lists Robert Glenister alongside Angela Bassett in "Close to the Enemy" and explicitly describes that series as "a British period drama miniseries set in the late 1940s in London" (quoted). Bridge entity: Robert Glenister. Evidence anchors to verify or extract the city: the "Close to the Enemy" article text ("set in the late 1940s in London") and its cast listing (Angela Bassett; Robert Glenister).

It is written and directed by Stephen Poliakoff, starring Jim Sturgess, Freddie Highmore, Charlotte Riley, Phoebe Fox, Alfred Molina, Lindsay Duncan, August Diehl, Alfie Allen, Angela Bassett, Antje Traue, Lily G and Robert Glenister.  It premiered in the United Kingdom on BBC Two on 10 November 2016.

Angela Bassett filmography | The following is a comprehensive list of acting and directing credits for American actress Angela Bassett.

Adam Bassett | Adam Bassett is a British lighting designer.  Bassett designs, directs and produces a wide range of lighting projects.  Bassett was the Associate Lighting Designer for the opening and closing ceremonies of the 2012 London Olympics and Paralympics.

London Has Fallen | London Has Fallen is a 2016 American action thriller film directed by Babak Najafi and written by Creighton Rothenberger, Katrin Benedikt, Chad St. John and Christian Gudegast.  It is a sequel to Antoine Fuqua's 2013 film "Olympus Has Fallen" and stars Gerard Butler, Aaron Eckhart and Morgan Freeman, with Alon Moni Aboutboul, Angela Bassett, Robert Forster, Jackie Earle Haley, Melissa Leo, Radha Mitchell, Sean O'Bryan, Waleed Zuaiter and Charlotte Riley in supporting roles.  It is the second installment in the "Has Fallen" film series.

Angela Bassett | Angela Evelyn Bassett (born August 16, 1958) is an American actress and activist.  She is best known for her biographical film roles, most notably her performance as Tina Turner in the biopic "What's Love Got to Do with It" (1993), for which she was nominated for the Academy Award for Best Actress and won a corresponding Golden Globe Award.  Bassett has additionally portrayed Betty Shabazz in both "Malcolm X" (1992) and "Panther" (1995), Katherine Jackson in "" (1992), Rosa Parks in "The Rosa Parks Story" (2002), Voletta Wallace in "Notorious" (2009) and Coretta Scott King in "Betty & Coretta" (2013).  Bassett's performance as Parks was honored with her first Primetime Emmy Award nomination.

Robert Glenister | Robert Lewis Glenister (born 11th March 1960) is an English actor known for his roles as con man Ash "Three Socks" Morgan in the BBC television series "Hustle" and Nicholas Blake in the BBC spy drama "Spooks".

response.summary_2: The miniseries (Close to the Enemy), which stars Robert Glenister alongside Angela Bassett, is set in London (in the late 1940s).
